name: Personal Information Protection Law of the People's Republic of China
summary: China's comprehensive personal information protection law, forming a pillar of China's data governance framework alongside the Cybersecurity Law and Data Security Law. It has extraterritorial scope and imposes strict requirements on cross-border data transfers, consent, and data localization.
keyRequirements: Obtain separate consent for sensitive personal information processing, cross-border transfers, and public disclosure
Conduct personal information protection impact assessments for high-risk processing activities
Appoint a personal information protection officer and register with authorities
Data localization requirements for critical information infrastructure operators and large-scale processors
Cross-border data transfer mechanisms: security assessment, standard contracts, or certification
Individual rights including access, correction, deletion, portability, and withdrawal of consent
Mandatory breach notification to authorities and affected individuals
penalties: For serious violations: fines up to RMB 50 million (~USD 7 million) or 5% of previous year's annual revenue, suspension or termination of business, revocation of business licenses. Individual liability: fines up to RMB 1 million. Enforced by the Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC).
